round1: Round 1 is in the books, and while everyone somehow managed to pick Colorado, the rest was a dumpster fire for most. Shoutout to Jaclyn for being the *only* one to see Buffalo's sweep coming, and Stephanie for sniffing out Boston, but Marc, you only got three teams right, at least you nailed two series, unlike Jaclyn who got five teams but somehow zero games. Good job, everyone, on making sure the bookies stay rich.
round2: Round 2 saw every series end in 6 games, a pattern only Jaclyn seemed to notice, netting three perfect picks. The rest of you mostly floundered, with Marc and Dad barely scoring a point, and Mom and Stephanie delivering a masterclass in futility with a pristine zero each. Maybe try picking teams that actually win next time.
round3: Round 3 is in the books! DAD nailed Buffalo in 5 and MOM got Dallas in 7, proving that sometimes even a broken clock is right. Jaclyn, however, managed to pick both series losers, a truly impressive feat of futility.
round4: The Dallas Stars are your 1999 Stanley Cup Champions, but don't ask anyone in this pool to explain how that happened. Every single one of you went all-in on Buffalo, proving once again that family loyalty doesn't win championships. A perfect record of wrong, folks.
overall: Alright, the 1999 NHL playoffs are mercifully over, and Derrick is officially your pool champion, cruising to 39 points by picking all eight teams and nailing five series lengths, a performance so dominant it almost makes up for the boredom of watching Dallas win. Jaclyn actually put up a decent fight for second with 35 points, just edging out Marc's 33, proving some of you at least tried. Then there's Stephanie, who scraped together a pathetic 17 points, managing a single correct series length—one!—leaving us to wonder if she even knows what a puck is. Frankly, most of you looked like amateurs, but Stephanie truly set a new low bar for futility.
